rationale: openapi.yaml is mostly HTTP/REST vocabulary. Under the v1.6.0 full-spec anchor, soft-spec fields like target_persona.primary_pain ('deadline-panic') would NEVER appear here and would blow the threshold. Under A-7 technical anchor (chosen + must_have_constraints.value only), the anchor stays narrow and the write's product-intent overlap is high enough to pass at 0.3.

incoming_content:
openapi: 3.1.0
info:
  title: Invoice Reconciliation API
  version: 1.0.0
  description: Reconciliation endpoints for freelance accountant clients with SOC2 audit logging
paths:
  /reconcile/invoices:
    post:
      summary: Reconcile a batch of invoices against bank feeds
      description: Accepts a list of invoice records and matches against connected bank feed transactions. Returns audit-ready diffs with latency under 500ms p95.
      requestBody:
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              properties:
                invoices:
                  type: array
                  items:
                    $ref: '#/components/schemas/Invoice'
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Reconciliation result with audit diffs
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/ReconciliationResult'
  /reconcile/status:
    get:
      summary: Poll reconciliation job status
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Job status
components:
  schemas:
    Invoice:
      type: object
      required: [id, amount, currency, vendor]
      properties:
        id: {type: string}
        amount: {type: number}
        currency: {type: string}
        vendor: {type: string}
        issued_date: {type: string, format: date}
        paid_date: {type: string, format: date}
    ReconciliationResult:
      type: object
      properties:
        matched: {type: array, items: {$ref: '#/components/schemas/Invoice'}}
        diffs: {type: array}
        audit_log_id: {type: string}

rationale: apps/api/README.md is build-instruction narrative. It paraphrases the product (reconciliation for freelance accountants) but also has lots of infra-only text (pnpm install, docker-compose up, port 3000) that under a chosen-only anchor would pass but could FP if the anchor got padded with every soft-spec field as a raw string. With A-7 narrative anchor (chosen + json.dumps(spec)), the full product vocabulary IS in the anchor but written as markdown narrative still beats threshold 0.4 because persona/pain/constraint terms are touched.

idea_spec.idea_summary: Invoice reconciliation API for freelance accountants

incoming_content:
# Invoice Reconciliation API

Product-facing REST service for freelance accountants who need to reconcile invoices across multiple banking providers before client deadlines. The API exposes audit-ready diffs so reconciliations are defensible during a SOC2 review and stay under 500ms p95.

## Who this is for

Freelance accountants dealing with tax-season deadline-panic. The primary workflow is importing invoice records, matching against bank feeds, and exporting an audit-ready diff set. Non-goals: general accounting UI, tax filing — see `specs/SPEC.md` for the full boundary.

## Getting started

```bash
pnpm install
cp .env.example .env
docker-compose up -d postgres
pnpm prisma migrate dev
pnpm --filter @app/api dev  # http://localhost:3000
```

## Endpoints

- `POST /reconcile/invoices` — batch reconciliation with audit diffs
- `GET  /reconcile/status`   — job-status polling
- `GET  /healthz`            — liveness

## Deployment

We use docker-compose for development and a container image built via `pnpm --filter @app/api build && docker build`. Production reconciliations are processed in real-time against connected bank feeds; the 500ms p95 constraint shapes the entire service. SOC2 audit logs are written for every request.

rationale: Same chosen_preview as above (invoice reconciliation for freelance accountants), but the incoming SPEC is a Slack bot for retail inventory. Almost no product-intent overlap — must BLOCK (exit 2) even under the looser technical threshold of 0.3.

openapi: 3.1.0
info:
  title: Retail Inventory Slack Bot
  version: 0.1.0
  description: Slack slash-command bot for brick-and-mortar boutique inventory tracking
paths:
  /slack/command:
    post:
      summary: Handle /inventory slash command
      description: Receives Slack slash-command payload, looks up boutique SKU stock levels, replies with a threaded Block Kit message
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Slack response attachment
  /slack/interactivity:
    post:
      summary: Handle Slack button clicks from inventory messages
      responses:
        '200':
          description: acknowledge
components:
  schemas:
    SlackPayload:
      type: object
      properties:
        team_id: {type: string}
        channel_id: {type: string}
        user_id: {type: string}
        text: {type: string}
    BoutiqueSKU:
      type: object
      properties:
        sku: {type: string}
        quantity_on_hand: {type: integer}
        reorder_threshold: {type: integer}
        supplier: {type: string}
